entry.text: The sky turns a colour you do not have a name for — somewhere between sickly yellow and the inside of a bruise. The air pressure changes. A sound begins: low, subsonic, felt more than heard. Then, with no further warning, it rains hotdogs. Perfectly preserved. Boiling hot. Falling in a dense curtain at terminal velocity from a sky that offers no explanation. They hit the ground and bounce. They hit you. The burns are immediate.

choices.label: Stand in it and collect as many as possible
Find minimal shelter and grab what lands nearby

hotdog_haul.text: The rain lasts four minutes and seventeen seconds — you count, because that's the kind of thing you do now. When it stops, the scrub is carpeted with them. They cool quickly and are, genuinely, fine. More than fine. They are completely, inexplicably, suspiciously fine. You don't ask what they're made of. You don't ask where they came from. You fill every available container.

severe_burns.text: Boiling mystery meat at terminal velocity, delivered to your exposed skin for four solid minutes. The blisters will tell this story for weeks.

minor_burns.text: A few hotdogs find gaps in your cover and leave their mark. The smell of your own skin cooking is hard to shake. You have had worse days, technically.
